expressions.english: to envy; to be jealous of
expressions.notes: -이/여/여지다 is used at the end of some adjectives to change them into verbs to express one's feeling or describe how one treats others.

expressions.english: to look at with envious eyes
You envy me (him/her), right?
I envy you.
I'm not particularly envious.
Don't be envious.
There is no need to be jealous.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ 필요 없다 = to not need to + V

expressions.english: Why are you envious of Hyunwoo?
expressions.notes: 왜 = why; for what reason; how come

expressions.english:
Aren't you jealous of Yoona?
Don't you envy Yoona?
Do you envy Hyojin that much?
My friends say they are jealous of me because I bought a new cellphone.
expressions.notes:
새 = new
핸드폰 = mobile phone
샀더니 = I heard that + S + V; They say + S + V

expressions.korean: 다른 건 안 부러운데 그거 하나는 부럽거든요.
expressions.english: I don't envy you, but that one thing, I am jealous of.
expressions.notes:
다른 = other; another
그거 = that
하나는 = one (native Korean number)

expressions.korean: 다른 사람의 성공을 너무 부러워하지 마세요.
expressions.english: Don't be too envious of another person's success.

expressions.korean: 저는 그림을 잘 그리는 사람이 너무 부러워요.
expressions.english: I really envy people that draw well.
expressions.notes:
그림 = drawing, painting, picture
그리다 = to draw, paint

expressions.korean: 그 일이 얼마나 편할까 생각하니 정말 부러워요.
expressions.english: As I think about how comfortable that work is, I am really jealous.
expressions.notes:
얼마나 = how + adjective/adverb
편하다 = to be comfortable; to be convenient
생각하다 = to think

expressions.korean: 그렇게 부러우면 윤아 씨도 그거 사면 되잖아요.
expressions.english: If you are that envious, Yoona, you can just buy it, too.
expressions.notes: -(으)면 되다 = to be just supposed to + V; to just have to + V; can just + V

expressions.korean: 저도 모르게 부러운 눈으로 그 사람을 쳐다봤어요.
expressions.english: I look at that person with envious eyes without even noticing it.
expressions.notes:
모르게 = without knowing; without meaning to + V
쳐다봤어요 = to look (at); to stare

expressions.korean: 하고 싶은 일을 하고 있는 사람들이 제일 부러워요.
expressions.english: I am most envious of people that are doing what they want to do.
expressions.notes:
일을 하다 = to work
-고 있다 = to be + V-ing
제일 = the best; the most

expressions.korean: 부러워하지 마세요, 현정 씨도 곧 대학생이 될 거예요.
expressions.english: Don't envy them. You, Hyunjeong, will also be a university student soon.
expressions.notes:
곧 = soon
대학생 = university student
되다 = to become

expressions.korean: 친구가 얼마 전에 로또에 당첨 됐는데 정말 부러웠어요.
expressions.english: My friend won that lottery a little while ago, and I was really jealous.
expressions.notes:
얼마 전 = a while ago; the other day
로또 = lotto, lottery
로또(에) 당첨 되다 = to win a lottery
